name: imagebind
paper_title: ImageBind: One Embedding Space To Bind Them All
authors: Rohit Girdhar, Alaaeldin El-Nouby, Zhuang Liu, Mannat Singh, Kalyan Vasudev Alwala, Armand Joulin, Ishan Misra
venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2023
license: Code + weights: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (facebookresearch/ImageBind LICENSE). Research-only, non-commercial.
notes: Zero-shot 'cosine tier' baseline (FROZEN). ImageBind binds 6 modalities (image/video, text, audio, depth, thermal, IMU) into one 1024-d space via contrastive alignment to the image tower. Zero-shot HAR = cosine similarity between a window's IMU-tower embedding and each candidate label string's text-tower embedding. IMU tower (18.69M params) trained on Ego4D HEAD-MOUNTED IMU via Meta's IMU2CLIP pipeline, so it transfers poorly to phone/watch HAR (UniMTS reports ~12.5% zero-shot acc / ~7.8 macro-F1) -- this is the 'generic multimodal binding doesn't transfer' reference floor, not a bug. INPUT CONTRACT (architecture + IMU2CLIP preprocessing; the released ImageBind repo ships NO load_and_transform_imu_data): IMU tensor (B,6,2000) = accel xyz + gyro xyz, 2000 steps; IMUPreprocessor(img_size=[6,2000], kernel_size=8) patchifies to 250 length-8 patches -> Linear(48) stem. 200 Hz, 2000 samples = 10 s (IMU2CLIP resampleIMU->200Hz, padIMU->round(dur)*200); accel in m/s^2 WITH gravity, gyro rad/s (Ego4D native units, NO per-sample normalization). Our grids store accel in g so x9.80665; gyro already rad/s. Short windows zero-padded to 2000, long ones cropped. INSTALL: pip 'imagebind' is a 1.1 kB empty stub; cloned github.com/facebookresearch/ImageBind into baselines/imagebind/repo/ (gitignored). Repo __init__ eagerly imports data.py -> pytorchvideo (broken on modern torchvision); adapter registers a sys.modules package stub to import model+tokenizer WITHOUT running __init__ (video/audio unneeded). Checkpoint imagebind_huge.pth (~4.5 GB) cached in repo/.checkpoints/ (gitignored). Extra deps installed in venv: iopath (portalocker). ImageBind postprocessors L2-norm then apply per-modality LearnableLogitScaling, so adapter re-L2-normalizes both towers for pure cosine.
